title: oxydectes santolina
intro: Oxydectes santolina, also known as the Santolina plant or Lavender Cotton or Cotton Lavender, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its grayish foliage and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Oxydectes santolina include relief from indigestion, bloating, and gas, as well as a reduced risk of heartburn and other gastrointestinal disorders. Oxydectes santolina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
